paper_title: Effect of different salinities on the susceptibility of striped catfish (Pangasianodon hypophthalmus) to Aeromonas hydrophila bacteria causing hemorrhagic disease

entities.description: The likelihood of striped catfish being infected by Aeromonas hydrophila bacteria causing disease.
The impact of different salt concentrations on fish health and disease resistance.
A bacterial disease in fish caused by Aeromonas hydrophila.
The primary research field of the paper.
Method of challenging fish with A. hydrophila bacteria.
Technique used for re-identification of A. hydrophila bacteria.
Mathematical formula used to determine the lethal dose of 50% (LD50).
Statistical test used for data analysis.
Culture medium used for bacterial growth.
Equipment used to determine bacterial density.
The lethal dose causing 50% mortality in the experimental fish.
Recorded mortality rate at freshwater salinity after 14 days.
Initial weight of the experimental striped catfish.

entities.id: College of Aquaculture and Fisheries (CAF), Can Tho University
